I read the attached prompt as fixing a very specific presentation format: first two generators are the tame generators ŌÉ,ŌĄ, the tame relation ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2 is implicit and should not be listed, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents inZ must be supplied modulo M=85667662080.The useful external boundary conditions are these. The pÓÄ†=2 case is known from Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg; Diekert covers a dyadic case where K(‚ąí1)/K is unramified, but that explicitly does not cover K=Q2. Jarden‚ÄďShusterman also record that Gal(K) has minimal abstract generator number [K:Qp]+2 and minimal relation number [K:Qp]+1, so for Q2 the minimal abstract numbers are 3 generators and 2 relations.TAU Mathematics The prompt-compatible tame presentation may still use four generators, as in the known p>2 style.The maximal pro-2 quotient is the most rigid check. The problem page states the useful presentationGQ2 (2)=‚ü®x,y,z‚ą£x2y3yz=1‚ü©,and Labute‚Äôs DemuŇ°kin classification gives the equivalent q=2,d=1 relation x12x24(x2,x3)=1 in his commutator convention.Epoch AI+1 Therefore any full tame presentation with ŌĄ killed and ŌÉ projected to its pro-2 part must reduce tox2y3yŌÉ=1.The new ansatz I isolated is to replace y in the last factor by the pro-2 component of yŌĄ. LetŌÄ2‚ąąZ be the idempotent with 2-adic coordinate 1 and all odd-primary coordinates 0. Modulo the verifier‚Äôs

M=85667662080,this isŌÄ2 ‚Č°40491355905(modM),since it is 1mod28 and 0 modulo the odd part of M. DefineNŌĄ (y):=(yŌĄ)ŌÄ2.

Then the candidate relation isx2y3NŌĄ(y)ŌÉ=1.

In the prompt‚Äôs submission syntax, the candidate would be:sigma,tau,x,y ytau = (y*tau)^40491355905 x^2*y^3*ytau^sigma This passes three immediate consistency checks.First, it lies over the tame quotient: setting x=y=1, we get (yŌĄ)ŌÄ2=ŌĄŌÄ2=1, since ŌĄ has prime-to-2 order. Second, in the maximal pro-2 quotient, ŌĄ=1, so NŌĄ (y)=y, and the relation becomes exactly x2y3yŌÉ=1. Third, after abelianizing the pro-2 quotient, it gives 2x+4y=0, hence Z22‚äēC2, matching the 2-primary abelianization expected from local class field theory for Q2√ó; Labute recalls the local-class-field identification of H1 with K\*/K \*p and the Hilbert-symbol cup product in this setting.Cambridge University Press & Assessment The most nontrivial check is over finite tame extensions. LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌĄňČŌÉňČ=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,where e is odd and e‚ą£2f‚ąí1. Schneider‚Äôs computation for the analogous dyadic norm term gives, modulo commutators,(x2,ŌĄ)‚Č°x2őĽ,őĽ=e‚ąí1i=0‚ąĎe‚ąí1ŌĄi,which is exactly the linear behavior of (yŌĄ)ŌÄ2.Regensburg Publications Over F2, e‚ąí1=1, so the linear part of the conjugates of my relation on the y-orbit is multiplication by1+NŌÉňČ,N=i=0‚ąĎe‚ąí1ŌĄňČi.

Now N is a central idempotent in F2[őĒe,f]: N2=eN=N, andŌÉňČ permutes the powers ofŌĄňČ. Thus (1‚ąíN)(1+NŌÉňČ)=1‚ąíN,so the augmentation-zero ŌĄ-part is killed; on the N-part the relation becomes 1+ŌÉňČ, leaving exactly one y-dimension. The x-orbit contributes ef mod-2 generators, and the unramified pro-2 generator contributes one more, givingd2=ef+2,which is exactly the DemuŇ°kin generator rank for a degree ef finite extension of Q2. Labute‚Äôs theorem gives this d+2 rank for local maximal pro-p Galois groups.Cambridge University Press & Assessment I am not claiming this is a completed presentation yet. The remaining proof has to show more than the Frattini-rank calculation: for every finite tame L/Q2, the pro-2 Sylow subgroup obtained from this presentation must have the correct DemuŇ°kin relation, including the nondegenerate cup product and the correct 2-adic cyclotomic character/Arf invariant. Schneider‚Äôs thesis emphasizes that exactly this dyadic ramified-i situation lacks the clean DemuŇ°kin-formation uniqueness argument available in the pÓÄ†=2 case.Regensburg Publications

ESTABLISHED: A concrete one-relation tame-compatible candidate was isolated:x2y3 ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ2)ŌÉ=1,ŌÄ2 ‚Č°40491355905(modM).It matches the known maximal pro-2 quotient, the abelianization check, and the H1 -rank ef+2 for all finite tame (e,f)-quotients.

GAP: The second-Zassenhaus/cup-product computation for every finite tame subextension is not yet done, so the candidate is not proved to recover the correct DemuŇ°kin group GL (2) in all cases.

NEXT: Compute the quadratic initial form of the transferred relator over F2[őĒe,f], reduce it to Labute normal form for ef odd and even, and verify the cyclotomic-character invariant.[BREAKTHROUGH]
